LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE JAN-06-2025 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'S MOTION TO VACATE DISMISSAL UNDER C.C.P. 664.6 & ENTER JUDGMENT PURSUANT TO STIPULATION; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. ALTHOUGH TIMELY FILED THE MOTION IS UNOPPOSED. STAY IN THIS MATTER IS VACATED PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 664.6. PURSUANT TO THE STIPULATION ENTERED INTO BY THE PARTIES, JUDGMENT IS ENTERED IN PLAINTIFF'S FAVOR IN THE AMOUNT OF $12,187.00, INCLUDING COURT COSTS IN THE AMOUNT OF $445.00. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: BARBARA A. ZUNIGA; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (302/BAZ)

Motion To Vacate Dismissal Under C.C.P. 664.6 & Enter Judgment Pursuant To Stipulation
Off Calendar Jan-06-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'s Motion To Vacate Dismissal Under C.C.P. 664.6 & Enter Judgment Pursuant To Stipulation.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Although timely filed the motion is unopposed. Stay in this matter is vacated pursuant to C.C.P. section 664.6. Pursuant to the stipulation entered into by the parties, judgment is entered in Plaintiff's favor in the amount of $12,187.00, including court costs in the amount of $445.00.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Barbara A. Zuniga; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/BAZ)

Motion To Vacate Dismissal Under Ccp 664.6 And Enter Judgmnt
Off Calendar Mar-13-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. JONATHAN VAKNIN, Esq. of Vaknin Law Firm (tel: 415-851-5297) specially appearing for Plaintiff.
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'s Motion To Vacate Dismissal Under Ccp 664.6 And Enter Judgment.

Matter discussed. Counsel Vaknin states the case has settled and requests a 60-day continuance. Upon conclusion of discussion, the Court takes the matter off calendar, as the case has settled. Plaintiff may refile the motion.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported.(302/RBU)
